Attorney General Bonta Announces $95 Million Settlement Against Meta Platforms for Data Breach

May 14, 2026

California Attorney General Rob Bonta today announced a $95 million settlement against Meta Platforms, Inc. resolving allegations that the company failed to protect consumer personal information following a 2023 data breach affecting 4.6 million California residents. The settlement requires Meta to overhaul its security practices and submit to five years of independent audits.

Attorney General Bonta Sues Amazon for Wage Theft Affecting Warehouse Workers

April 28, 2026

Attorney General Rob Bonta today filed a lawsuit against Amazon.com Services LLC alleging systematic wage theft at California fulfillment centers. The complaint, filed in Alameda County Superior Court, alleges Amazon failed to pay overtime for time spent passing through security screenings, in violation of California Labor Code.

Attorney General Bonta Secures $12 Million Judgment Against Walmart Stores Inc. for Hazardous Waste Violations

April 11, 2026

California Attorney General Rob Bonta today secured a $12 million judgment against Walmart Stores Inc. for illegally disposing of hazardous waste — including pesticides, aerosol products, batteries, and electronic devices — at municipal landfills across California in violation of state environmental and consumer protection laws.
